categories.name: Recovery & Repair
categories.description: Tissue repair and recovery peptides accelerate healing at the cellular level — from muscles and tendons to gut lining and connective tissue.
categories.hero_description: The most studied healing peptides in research science. BPC-157, TB-500, and KPV have accumulated thousands of studies demonstrating unprecedented tissue repair capabilities.
categories.seo_description: Explore the top research recovery and tissue repair peptides including BPC-157 and TB-500. High-purity compounds for research use only.
categories.content:
Recovery peptides represent some of the most remarkable discoveries in peptide research science. These compounds work at the cellular level to accelerate healing processes that normally take weeks or months, making them invaluable tools for researchers studying tissue regeneration.

## How Recovery Peptides Work

Recovery peptides operate through multiple mechanisms. BPC-157, the gold standard in this category, promotes angiogenesis (new blood vessel formation), upregulates growth hormone receptors, and activates the nitric oxide system — all critical pathways for tissue repair.

TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4) works systemically by promoting actin polymerization, cell migration, and differentiation. Its smaller size relative to the full TB4 molecule allows it to circulate throughout the body, reaching damaged tissue wherever it exists.

## Research Applications

The research applications for recovery peptides are extensive. Current studies are exploring their potential in:
- Muscle and tendon injury models
- Gastrointestinal inflammation and repair
- Spinal cord injury recovery
- Cardiovascular tissue protection
- Chronic wound healing

## Purity Standards

For recovery peptide research, purity is paramount. Our featured recovery peptides are sourced from suppliers maintaining ≥99% purity standards, verified by HPLC analysis and mass spectrometry.

faq.question: What is the difference between BPC-157 and TB-500?
faq.answer: BPC-157 works more locally at the site of injury through direct cellular mechanisms, while TB-500 acts systemically throughout the body. Many researchers study both compounds together for complementary repair mechanisms.

faq.question: Are recovery peptides third-party tested?
faq.answer: Yes, all peptides listed on PeptidesMaxxing are sourced from suppliers that provide HPLC (High-Performance Liquid Chromatography) certificates of analysis and mass spectrometry verification.

faq.question: What storage conditions do recovery peptides require?
faq.answer: Recovery peptides in lyophilized (freeze-dried) form should be stored at -20°C for long-term storage (up to 2 years) or at 4°C for short-term use (up to 3 months). Keep away from light and moisture.

faq.question: Can recovery peptides be used in combination?
faq.answer: Combining BPC-157 and TB-500 is one of the most studied peptide protocols. Research suggests they work synergistically — BPC-157 provides local tissue repair while TB-500 provides systemic healing support.

faq.question: What is the research status of recovery peptides?
faq.answer: BPC-157 and TB-500 are research chemicals with substantial preclinical evidence. They are not FDA-approved for human use and are sold for research purposes only. Multiple phase I/II clinical trials are ongoing.

categories.name: Growth Hormone Peptides
categories.description: GH secretagogues and GHRH analogues that stimulate natural pituitary growth hormone release for research into body composition, recovery, and anti-aging.
categories.hero_description: The most advanced growth hormone research peptides available. From selective secretagogues like Ipamorelin to powerful GHRH analogues like CJC-1295 — science-grade compounds for GH axis research.
categories.seo_description: Top research growth hormone peptides including Ipamorelin, CJC-1295, Sermorelin, GHRP-2, and GHRP-6. High-purity GH secretagogues for research use only.
categories.content:
Growth hormone peptides represent a sophisticated class of research compounds that interact with the hypothalamic-pituitary axis to stimulate natural growth hormone secretion. Unlike synthetic HGH, these peptides work with the body's own regulatory mechanisms.

## Categories of GH Peptides

### GHRH Analogues
Peptides like Sermorelin, CJC-1295 (with and without DAC), and Modified GRF 1-29 mimic endogenous Growth Hormone-Releasing Hormone (GHRH). They bind to GHRH receptors on the pituitary to stimulate GH synthesis and release.

### GH Secretagogues (GHRPs)
GHRP-2, GHRP-6, Ipamorelin, and Hexarelin act on ghrelin receptors (GHS-R1a) to trigger GH release. Each has a distinct profile regarding selectivity, potency, and side effects.

## Synergistic Combinations

Combining a GHRH analogue with a GHRP creates synergistic GH release that far exceeds either compound alone. The most studied combination in research is Ipamorelin + CJC-1295 Without DAC, which produces a strong, clean GH pulse mimicking natural release patterns.

## Research Applications

GH peptide research encompasses body composition (muscle/fat), recovery enhancement, sleep quality improvements, bone density, anti-aging pathways, and metabolic health investigations.

faq.question: What is the difference between GHRPs and GHRH analogues?
faq.answer: GHRH analogues (CJC-1295, Sermorelin) bind to GHRH receptors and stimulate GH synthesis and release. GHRPs (Ipamorelin, GHRP-2, GHRP-6) bind to ghrelin receptors (GHS-R1a) and trigger GH release through a different pathway. Combining both creates synergistic GH release.

faq.question: Why is Ipamorelin considered the 'cleanest' GH peptide?
faq.answer: Ipamorelin selectively stimulates GH release without significantly affecting cortisol, prolactin, ACTH, or other hormones — unlike GHRP-2 and GHRP-6 which can elevate cortisol and prolactin. This makes it valuable for research requiring isolated GH stimulation.

faq.question: What's the difference between CJC-1295 with and without DAC?
faq.answer: CJC-1295 Without DAC (Modified GRF 1-29) has a half-life of approximately 30 minutes, producing a natural pulsatile GH release. CJC-1295 With DAC has a 6-8 day half-life via albumin binding, providing sustained GH elevation with less frequent dosing.

faq.question: Which GH peptide produces the largest GH pulse?
faq.answer: Hexarelin produces the largest GH pulse amplitude of any single synthetic GH secretagogue. However, it also desensitizes receptors more quickly than other GHRPs. GHRP-2 produces the second largest pulse, while Ipamorelin produces a more moderate but highly selective release.

faq.question: Are GH peptides legal to purchase for research?
faq.answer: GH peptides are legally sold as research chemicals in the United States for laboratory research purposes. They are not approved for human use by the FDA. Purchasing for non-research purposes or human consumption may violate applicable laws.

categories.name: Anti-Aging & Longevity
categories.description: Cutting-edge longevity peptides backed by decades of research — targeting telomeres, mitochondria, and cellular aging pathways for unprecedented anti-aging research.
categories.hero_description: The frontier of longevity science. Epithalon, GHK-Cu, and SS-31 represent the most advanced anti-aging peptide research — targeting the fundamental mechanisms of cellular aging.
categories.seo_description: Top anti-aging and longevity peptides including Epithalon, GHK-Cu, SS-31, and Thymosin Alpha-1. Premium purity compounds for longevity research.
categories.content:
Anti-aging peptides represent the cutting edge of longevity science — compounds that directly target the fundamental mechanisms of cellular aging, from telomere shortening to mitochondrial dysfunction.

## The Science of Peptide-Based Anti-Aging

Aging occurs through multiple interconnected mechanisms: telomere erosion, mitochondrial decline, stem cell exhaustion, cellular senescence, and disrupted intercellular communication. Anti-aging peptides offer a targeted approach to address each of these pathways.

## Key Longevity Peptides

### Epithalon — The Telomere Peptide
With over 35 years of research by Dr. Vladimir Khavinson, Epithalon remains the most studied peptide for telomere biology. Research demonstrates its ability to activate telomerase and elongate telomeres in human somatic cells — a process previously thought to be impossible.

### GHK-Cu — The Regenerative Peptide
Naturally declining from 200ng/mL at age 20 to 80ng/mL by age 60, GHK-Cu activates over 4,000 genes involved in tissue repair, anti-inflammation, and antioxidant defense. Its copper complex is essential for collagen, elastin, and skin regeneration.

### SS-31 — The Mitochondrial Peptide
By targeting cardiolipin in the inner mitochondrial membrane, SS-31 dramatically reduces reactive oxygen species production while restoring mitochondrial membrane potential — addressing the core of age-related energy decline.

faq.question: How does Epithalon extend telomeres?
faq.answer: Epithalon activates telomerase, the enzyme responsible for adding telomeric repeat sequences to chromosome ends. Research by Khavinson et al. demonstrated Epithalon's ability to elongate telomeres in human embryonic kidney cells and in aging human blood cells — a breakthrough in longevity research.

faq.question: What is the significance of GHK-Cu declining with age?
faq.answer: GHK-Cu plasma levels drop from ~200ng/mL at age 20 to ~80ng/mL by age 60. This 60% decline correlates with reduced collagen production, slower wound healing, and decreased stem cell activity. Research suggests maintaining GHK-Cu levels may counteract multiple aging biomarkers.

faq.question: How does SS-31 differ from other mitochondrial antioxidants?
faq.answer: Unlike general antioxidants (CoQ10, MitoQ), SS-31 specifically targets cardiolipin in the inner mitochondrial membrane. This selective targeting allows it to reduce mitochondrial ROS at the source while also preserving the electron transport chain structure — making it fundamentally different from systemic antioxidants.

faq.question: Can longevity peptides be studied in combination?
faq.answer: Multi-pathway longevity protocols are an active area of research. Combining Epithalon (telomere support) + GHK-Cu (gene activation/repair) + SS-31 (mitochondrial function) theoretically addresses the three major hallmarks of aging: genomic instability, mitochondrial dysfunction, and altered intercellular communication.

faq.question: What does the research say about Epithalon's lifespan effects?
faq.answer: Animal studies by Khavinson demonstrated significant lifespan extension in mice and rats treated with Epithalon. More importantly, multiple studies in aging humans showed improvements in antioxidant status, melatonin levels, and reduced cancer incidence in elderly subjects over a 12-year follow-up period.

categories.name: Cognitive & Nootropic
categories.description: Clinically-developed nootropic peptides for advanced cognitive research — enhancing BDNF, promoting neurogenesis, and supporting memory, focus, and neuroprotection.
categories.hero_description: The most advanced cognitive peptides ever developed. Semax, Selank, and Dihexa emerged from decades of Russian neuroscience — representing a new paradigm in nootropic research.
categories.seo_description: Top cognitive and nootropic peptides including Semax, Selank, and Dihexa. Research-grade compounds for memory, focus, and neuroprotection studies.
categories.content:
Cognitive peptides represent a paradigm shift in nootropic science — moving beyond simple neurotransmitter manipulation to directly influencing neuroplasticity, neurogenesis, and brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) pathways.

## The Neuroscience Behind Cognitive Peptides

Unlike traditional nootropics (racetams, stimulants) that work primarily on neurotransmitter systems, cognitive peptides operate at the level of gene expression and protein synthesis — creating structural changes in neural architecture rather than temporary chemical shifts.

## Key Mechanisms

### BDNF Upregulation
Semax elevates BDNF by up to 800% in hippocampal tissue in animal studies. BDNF is essential for long-term potentiation (memory formation), neuronal survival, and synaptic plasticity — making its upregulation one of the most impactful cognitive interventions known.

### Synaptogenesis (Dihexa)
Dihexa promotes the formation of new synaptic connections at a potency estimated to be 10 million times greater than BDNF itself. Studies at Washington State University demonstrated Dihexa's ability to reverse cognitive deficits in Alzheimer's disease models.

### Anxiolytic-Nootropic Duality
Selank uniquely combines anxiolytic properties with cognitive enhancement — reducing anxiety through GABA-A modulation while simultaneously upregulating BDNF and improving neurotransmitter balance.

faq.question: How does Semax compare to traditional nootropics?
faq.answer: Traditional nootropics primarily modulate neurotransmitter levels (dopamine, acetylcholine, GABA) for short-term cognitive effects. Semax works by upregulating BDNF and activating cognitive genes, producing structural neuroplasticity changes — fundamentally more powerful and longer-lasting than neurotransmitter-based approaches.

faq.question: What makes Dihexa so extraordinary as a cognitive compound?
faq.answer: Dihexa's potency for inducing synaptogenesis is estimated at 10^7 (10 million) times greater than BDNF itself. In Alzheimer's models, it not only stopped cognitive decline but reversed existing deficits — something few compounds have demonstrated. Its small size also allows it to cross the blood-brain barrier efficiently.

faq.question: Is Selank truly anxiolytic without sedation or dependency?
faq.answer: Research supports Selank's anxiolytic effects through GABA-A receptor modulation and enkephalin regulation, but crucially without the sedation, tolerance, or dependency associated with benzodiazepines. Multiple studies in anxious human subjects showed significant anxiety reduction with concurrent cognitive improvement.

faq.question: Can Semax and Selank be used together in research?
faq.answer: Combining Semax (BDNF upregulation, focus, memory) with Selank (anxiety reduction, GABA balance) is a well-studied approach. The complementary mechanisms — Semax for cognitive performance, Selank for anxiety modulation — create a comprehensive nootropic protocol in research models.

faq.question: What is the research backing for peptide nootropics?
faq.answer: Semax and Selank have been used clinically in Russia for decades, with hundreds of peer-reviewed publications. Dihexa has been studied at WSU with peer-reviewed publications in major journals. These are among the most research-validated nootropic compounds available.

categories.name: Body Composition
categories.description: Research peptides targeting fat loss and body composition optimization — with TGA-approved mechanisms and rigorous clinical backing.
categories.hero_description: Science-backed body composition peptides. AOD-9604 carries TGA approval in Australia and represents the gold standard in fat-targeted peptide research.
categories.seo_description: Top body composition peptides including AOD-9604 and Melanotan II for fat loss research. TGA-approved mechanisms with high-purity compounds.
categories.content:
Body composition peptides target fat metabolism through specific receptor pathways, offering a more targeted approach than systemic hormones or stimulants. The most notable is AOD-9604, which carries TGA (Australian Therapeutic Goods Administration) approval.

## AOD-9604 — The Fat-Targeting Peptide

Developed at Monash University by Professor Frank Ng, AOD-9604 is a modified fragment of human growth hormone (amino acids 176-191 with a tyrosine addition). Crucially, it retains the fat-metabolism properties of hGH without affecting IGF-1 levels — eliminating the anabolic and carcinogenic concerns associated with full HGH therapy.

## Mechanism of Action

AOD-9604 stimulates lipolysis (fat breakdown) and inhibits lipogenesis (fat formation) through direct stimulation of beta-3 adrenergic receptors and modulation of GH receptor signaling in adipose tissue. Importantly, it does not bind to growth hormone receptors in non-adipose tissue.

## Clinical Development

AOD-9604 underwent phase I, II, and III clinical trials for obesity treatment. While it didn't meet the high bar for FDA approval as a pharmaceutical, the trial data established its excellent safety profile and demonstrated clinically meaningful fat loss in obese subjects.

faq.question: What makes AOD-9604 different from full HGH for fat loss?
faq.answer: AOD-9604 contains only the fat-metabolizing fragment of HGH (amino acids 176-191). It does not bind GH receptors in non-adipose tissue, meaning it doesn't raise IGF-1, doesn't cause insulin resistance, and has no anabolic effects — making it significantly safer than full HGH for fat loss research.

faq.question: What does TGA approval mean for AOD-9604?
faq.answer: TGA (Therapeutic Goods Administration) is Australia's equivalent of the FDA. AOD-9604's TGA approval confirms it has passed rigorous safety evaluations in clinical trials. This regulatory recognition is exceptional for a peptide and provides strong confidence in its safety profile for research purposes.

faq.question: How does Melanotan II affect body composition?
faq.answer: Melanotan II activates MC4 receptors in the hypothalamus, which regulates appetite and metabolism. Research shows significant appetite suppression and increased energy expenditure through MC4R signaling — effects entirely separate from its tanning and libido mechanisms.

faq.question: Can AOD-9604 and growth hormone peptides be combined?
faq.answer: Research has explored combining AOD-9604 with GH secretagogues for comprehensive body composition protocols. The rationale is that AOD-9604 targets adipose tissue directly while GHRPs/GHRH analogues support muscle preservation and metabolic optimization.

faq.question: What was the outcome of AOD-9604 clinical trials?
faq.answer: Phase II trials in 300+ obese subjects showed statistically significant fat loss versus placebo. Phase III trials expanded to 900+ subjects with promising results. While the drug didn't achieve FDA approval (requiring extreme efficacy for a pharmaceutical), the trials established an excellent safety record with no serious adverse events.

categories.name: Sexual Health
categories.description: FDA-approved and clinically validated melanocortin peptides for sexual health research — working through central nervous system pathways for a unique mechanism of action.
categories.hero_description: Centrally-acting sexual health research peptides with FDA-approved mechanisms. PT-141 represents a paradigm shift in sexual health science — working through the brain, not the cardiovascular system.
categories.seo_description: Top sexual health peptides including PT-141 and Melanotan II. FDA-approved mechanisms with research-grade purity for sexual health research.
categories.content:
Sexual health peptides represent a fundamentally different approach to sexual dysfunction research — working through the central nervous system rather than the cardiovascular system (like PDE5 inhibitors such as Viagra and Cialis).

## The Melanocortin System

Both PT-141 and Melanotan II act on melanocortin receptors (MC3R and MC4R) in the central nervous system, particularly in the hypothalamus. These receptors are directly involved in regulating sexual arousal and desire — creating effects that are qualitatively different from vascular-based approaches.

## PT-141 (Bremelanotide) — FDA Approved

PT-141 received FDA approval as Vyleesi (bremelanotide injection, 1.75mg) for hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD) in premenopausal women. This makes it one of only a handful of research peptides with full FDA approval, providing extraordinary validation for its safety and efficacy in human subjects.

## Research Applications

Beyond its FDA-approved use in women, research has explored PT-141's applications in male sexual dysfunction, its potential in libido disorders resistant to conventional treatments, and the basic neuroscience of melanocortin-mediated arousal pathways.

faq.question: How does PT-141 differ from Viagra or Cialis?
faq.answer: Viagra and Cialis are PDE5 inhibitors that work by increasing blood flow to the genitals through cardiovascular pathways. PT-141 works through the central nervous system by activating MC3R and MC4R receptors in the hypothalamus — enhancing desire and arousal at the brain level, not the vascular level. This makes it effective even when blood flow is not the primary issue.

faq.question: What is Vyleesi and how does it relate to PT-141?
faq.answer: Vyleesi is the FDA-approved pharmaceutical form of bremelanotide (PT-141), approved in June 2019 for hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD) in premenopausal women. Research-grade PT-141 uses the same active compound (bremelanotide) at the same concentration, providing researchers with access to the same molecule that passed FDA approval.

faq.question: What is the difference between PT-141 and Melanotan II for sexual health research?
faq.answer: Both act on melanocortin receptors, but PT-141 (cyclic hexapeptide) is more selective for MC3R and MC4R, giving a cleaner sexual arousal response. Melanotan II (cyclic heptapeptide) has broader receptor activity including MC1R (tanning) and MC4R (appetite suppression), making it a multi-effect compound with sexual arousal as one of several research endpoints.

faq.question: Can PT-141 be researched for male subjects?
faq.answer: Yes. Clinical trials have studied PT-141 in men with erectile dysfunction, particularly those who don't respond to PDE5 inhibitors. Results showed significant improvements in erectile function through the central arousal pathway. Research is ongoing for male hypoactive sexual desire disorder.

faq.question: What are the established safety parameters for PT-141 from clinical trials?
faq.answer: FDA approval trials established PT-141's primary adverse effects as nausea (40% of subjects), flushing (20%), and transient blood pressure increases. These are well-characterized and manageable. The FDA approval provides one of the most comprehensive safety profiles available for any research peptide.

categories.name: Anti-Inflammatory
categories.description: Targeted anti-inflammatory peptides that modulate key inflammatory pathways — from NF-κB inhibition to gut mucosal repair — without systemic immunosuppression.
categories.hero_description: Precision anti-inflammatory peptide research. BPC-157, KPV, and Thymosin Alpha-1 represent targeted approaches to inflammation modulation that don't compromise immune function.
categories.seo_description: Top anti-inflammatory peptides including BPC-157, KPV, and Thymosin Alpha-1. Research-grade compounds for inflammation modulation research.
categories.content:
Anti-inflammatory peptides offer a sophisticated approach to inflammation research that goes beyond simple COX inhibition. By targeting specific inflammatory signaling pathways, these peptides can modulate inflammation with precision that systemic anti-inflammatory drugs cannot match.

## Targeted Mechanisms

### NF-κB Pathway Inhibition
KPV directly inhibits NF-κB (Nuclear Factor kappa-light-chain-enhancer of activated B cells) — the master regulator of inflammatory gene expression. This single target controls the production of TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6, and dozens of other pro-inflammatory cytokines.

### Mucosal Healing
BPC-157 demonstrates remarkable effects on gastrointestinal mucosa, healing stomach ulcers, IBD models, and intestinal fistulas in research. It appears to work partly through upregulation of growth hormone receptors in intestinal cells and direct cytoprotective effects.

### Immune Modulation
Thymosin Alpha-1's anti-inflammatory properties are immunomodulatory rather than immunosuppressive — it enhances regulatory immune functions while reducing excessive pro-inflammatory activity.

faq.question: How does KPV inhibit inflammation differently from NSAIDs?
faq.answer: NSAIDs (ibuprofen, aspirin) inhibit COX enzymes to reduce prostaglandin synthesis — a downstream effect. KPV works upstream by inhibiting NF-κB, the master transcription factor for inflammatory gene expression. This means KPV can reduce many more pro-inflammatory mediators simultaneously, and through a pathway that NSAIDs don't touch.

faq.question: What inflammatory conditions has BPC-157 been studied in?
faq.answer: BPC-157 has been studied in models of IBD (Crohn's, colitis), stomach ulcers, periodontal disease, multiple sclerosis, rheumatoid arthritis, and systemic inflammation. Its anti-inflammatory effects appear to be systemic and multi-pathway, making it one of the most versatile anti-inflammatory peptides in research.

faq.question: Is Thymosin Alpha-1's anti-inflammatory effect separate from its immune-boosting effect?
faq.answer: These effects are interconnected. TA-1's immunomodulatory action shifts the immune response from Th2 (pro-inflammatory, allergic) towards Th1 (cellular immunity, anti-viral). This shift reduces chronic low-grade inflammation while enhancing appropriate immune responses — a true immunomodulation rather than immunosuppression.

faq.question: Can anti-inflammatory peptides be used in gut research?
faq.answer: Absolutely — gut inflammation is one of the strongest research applications for this category. BPC-157 has shown exceptional results in IBD models. KPV's NF-κB inhibition is particularly relevant for gut inflammation since NF-κB is hyperactivated in Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis. Research suggests oral delivery of KPV may be possible, unlike most peptides.

faq.question: Do anti-inflammatory peptides cause immune suppression?
faq.answer: Unlike corticosteroids or DMARDs, none of the anti-inflammatory peptides (BPC-157, KPV, TA-1) cause immune suppression. They work through targeted pathway modulation. TA-1 actually enhances immune function while reducing inflammation, and BPC-157's anti-inflammatory effects don't impair wound healing or infection resistance.
